我受国际站客户经理所托，根据她的客户的产品手册，是实物手册，制作ppt文件，文件中需要展示几个同行店铺的首页的数据（做了多少年，180天的销售额等）。做这个文件的目的就是国际站客户经理为了转化客户，给客户做一个市场分析。你了解我的意思吗，请使用brainstorming 分析这个方案</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-05-04</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on durable technical signal:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)
- Concrete debugging or investigative findings from logs, traces, queue state, database rows, and code-path inspection

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored, discovered, confirmed, traced

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built or learned):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"
- "Observation queue for claude-mem session timed out waiting for an agent pool slot"
- "Fallback processing abandoned pending messages after Gemini and OpenRouter returned 404"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings with no follow-on finding
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- File related research that comes back empty or not found

If skipping, return an empty response only. Do not explain the skip in prose.

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision | security_alert | security_note ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>ExitPlanMode</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-05-04T10:07:44.761Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>d:\ClaudeWorkspace</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"allowedPrompts\":[{\"tool\":\"Bash\",\"prompt\":\"install python-pptx\"},{\"tool\":\"Bash\",\"prompt\":\"run python script to generate PPTX\"},{\"tool\":\"Bash\",\"prompt\":\"web search via WebSearch/WebFetch for industry data\"}],\"plan\":\"# 医疗治疗仪行业·阿里国际站市场分析PPT\\n\\n## 背景\\n\\n- **客户行业**：医疗治疗仪（激光/红蓝光/经颅磁电治疗仪、检测仪、配件）\\n- **客户状态**：尚未入驻阿里国际站\\n- **使用场景**：客户经理上门面对面展示\\n- **PPT定位**：客观行业市场分析报告（不露转化意图）\\n- **语言**：中文\\n\\n## 数据来源\\n\\n| 数据类型 | 来源 | 谁做 |\\n|---------|------|:---:|\\n| 竞品店铺公开信息 | alibaba.com 搜索 | 我（WebSearch + WebFetch） |\\n| 竞品GMV数据 | 店铺名片悬停卡片 | 用户截图 |\\n| 行业趋势/地域分布 | 公开报告+海关数据 | 我 |\\n| 平台方向 | 阿里国际站官方 | 我 |\\n\\n## PPT结构（11页，中文）\\n\\n### Part 1：市场机会\\n1. 封面 — 医疗治疗仪行业·阿里国际站市场分析\\n2. 核心摘要 — 关键发现一页概览\\n3. 品类机会分析 — 子类目搜索热度/竞争度对比\\n4. 全球买家地域分析 — Top采购国分布\\n\\n### Part 2：竞争格局\\n5. 竞品总览 — 类目店铺概况\\n6-8. 对标店铺分析（3-4家各一页） — 截图占位+数据解读\\n9. 竞品汇总对比表\\n\\n### Part 3：行业前景\\n10. 国际站医疗行业趋势 — 平台方向/增长数据\\n11. 市场机遇总结\\n\\n## 执行流程\\n\\n### 阶段1：数据搜索（accio work 三路并行）\\n\\n```\\n                    ┌─ 路1：竞品店铺搜索 ──────→ 竞品数据\\n主任务 ── multi-* ──┼─ 路2：行业趋势+地域数据 ──→ 市场数据\\n                    └─ 路3：平台政策+医疗方向 ──→ 平台数据\\n```\\n\\n三路并行搜索，互不依赖，汇总后统一写PPT。\\n\\n### 阶段2：PPT生成\\n用 python-pptx 将汇总数据生成 11 页中文PPT，截图位置留占位框。\\n\\n### 阶段3：用户补充\\n用户打开PPT → 拖入竞品名片截图 → 完成。\\n\\n## 产出\\n\\n`D:\\\\ClaudeWorkspace\\\\05_PPT\\\\医疗治疗仪_国际站市场分析.pptx`\\n\\n## 验证\\n\\n- 11页完整 / 占位框标注清晰 / 中文表达 / 数据有出处\\n\",\"planFilePath\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\Administrator\\\\.claude\\\\plans\\\\ppt-180-brainstorming-playful-finch.md\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"plan\":\"# 医疗治疗仪行业·阿里国际站市场分析PPT\\n\\n## 背景\\n\\n- **客户行业**：医疗治疗仪（激光/红蓝光/经颅磁电治疗仪、检测仪、配件）\\n- **客户状态**：尚未入驻阿里国际站\\n- **使用场景**：客户经理上门面对面展示\\n- **PPT定位**：客观行业市场分析报告（不露转化意图）\\n- **语言**：中文\\n\\n## 数据来源\\n\\n| 数据类型 | 来源 | 谁做 |\\n|---------|------|:---:|\\n| 竞品店铺公开信息 | alibaba.com 搜索 | 我（WebSearch + WebFetch） |\\n| 竞品GMV数据 | 店铺名片悬停卡片 | 用户截图 |\\n| 行业趋势/地域分布 | 公开报告+海关数据 | 我 |\\n| 平台方向 | 阿里国际站官方 | 我 |\\n\\n## PPT结构（11页，中文）\\n\\n### Part 1：市场机会\\n1. 封面 — 医疗治疗仪行业·阿里国际站市场分析\\n2. 核心摘要 — 关键发现一页概览\\n3. 品类机会分析 — 子类目搜索热度/竞争度对比\\n4. 全球买家地域分析 — Top采购国分布\\n\\n### Part 2：竞争格局\\n5. 竞品总览 — 类目店铺概况\\n6-8. 对标店铺分析（3-4家各一页） — 截图占位+数据解读\\n9. 竞品汇总对比表\\n\\n### Part 3：行业前景\\n10. 国际站医疗行业趋势 — 平台方向/增长数据\\n11. 市场机遇总结\\n\\n## 执行流程\\n\\n### 阶段1：数据搜索（accio work 三路并行）\\n\\n```\\n                    ┌─ 路1：竞品店铺搜索 ──────→ 竞品数据\\n主任务 ── multi-* ──┼─ 路2：行业趋势+地域数据 ──→ 市场数据\\n                    └─ 路3：平台政策+医疗方向 ──→ 平台数据\\n```\\n\\n三路并行搜索，互不依赖，汇总后统一写PPT。\\n\\n### 阶段2：PPT生成\\n用 python-pptx 将汇总数据生成 11 页中文PPT，截图位置留占位框。\\n\\n### 阶段3：用户补充\\n用户打开PPT → 拖入竞品名片截图 → 完成。\\n\\n## 产出\\n\\n`D:\\\\ClaudeWorkspace\\\\05_PPT\\\\医疗治疗仪_国际站市场分析.pptx`\\n\\n## 验证\\n\\n- 11页完整 / 占位框标注清晰 / 中文表达 / 数据有出处\\n\",\"isAgent\":false,\"filePath\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\Administrator\\\\.claude\\\\plans\\\\ppt-180-brainstorming-playful-finch.md\",\"planWasEdited\":true}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Return either one or more <observation>...</observation> blocks, or an empty response if this tool use should be skipped.
Concrete debugging findings from logs, queue state, database rows, session routing, or code-path inspection count as durable discoveries and should be recorded.
Never reply with prose such as "Skipping", "No substantive tool executions", or any explanation outside XML. Non-XML text is discarded.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>ExitPlanMode</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-05-04T10:07:44.761Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>d:\ClaudeWorkspace</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"allowedPrompts\":[{\"tool\":\"Bash\",\"prompt\":\"install python-pptx\"},{\"tool\":\"Bash\",\"prompt\":\"run python script to generate PPTX\"},{\"tool\":\"Bash\",\"prompt\":\"web search via WebSearch/WebFetch for industry data\"}],\"plan\":\"# 医疗治疗仪行业·阿里国际站市场分析PPT\\n\\n## 背景\\n\\n- **客户行业**：医疗治疗仪（激光/红蓝光/经颅磁电治疗仪、检测仪、配件）\\n- **客户状态**：尚未入驻阿里国际站\\n- **使用场景**：客户经理上门面对面展示\\n- **PPT定位**：客观行业市场分析报告（不露转化意图）\\n- **语言**：中文\\n\\n## 数据来源\\n\\n| 数据类型 | 来源 | 谁做 |\\n|---------|------|:---:|\\n| 竞品店铺公开信息 | alibaba.com 搜索 | 我（WebSearch + WebFetch） |\\n| 竞品GMV数据 | 店铺名片悬停卡片 | 用户截图 |\\n| 行业趋势/地域分布 | 公开报告+海关数据 | 我 |\\n| 平台方向 | 阿里国际站官方 | 我 |\\n\\n## PPT结构（11页，中文）\\n\\n### Part 1：市场机会\\n1. 封面 — 医疗治疗仪行业·阿里国际站市场分析\\n2. 核心摘要 — 关键发现一页概览\\n3. 品类机会分析 — 子类目搜索热度/竞争度对比\\n4. 全球买家地域分析 — Top采购国分布\\n\\n### Part 2：竞争格局\\n5. 竞品总览 — 类目店铺概况\\n6-8. 对标店铺分析（3-4家各一页） — 截图占位+数据解读\\n9. 竞品汇总对比表\\n\\n### Part 3：行业前景\\n10. 国际站医疗行业趋势 — 平台方向/增长数据\\n11. 市场机遇总结\\n\\n## 执行流程\\n\\n### 阶段1：数据搜索（accio work 三路并行）\\n\\n```\\n                    ┌─ 路1：竞品店铺搜索 ──────→ 竞品数据\\n主任务 ── multi-* ──┼─ 路2：行业趋势+地域数据 ──→ 市场数据\\n                    └─ 路3：平台政策+医疗方向 ──→ 平台数据\\n```\\n\\n三路并行搜索，互不依赖，汇总后统一写PPT。\\n\\n### 阶段2：PPT生成\\n用 python-pptx 将汇总数据生成 11 页中文PPT，截图位置留占位框。\\n\\n### 阶段3：用户补充\\n用户打开PPT → 拖入竞品名片截图 → 完成。\\n\\n## 产出\\n\\n`D:\\\\ClaudeWorkspace\\\\05_PPT\\\\医疗治疗仪_国际站市场分析.pptx`\\n\\n## 验证\\n\\n- 11页完整 / 占位框标注清晰 / 中文表达 / 数据有出处\\n\",\"planFilePath\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\Administrator\\\\.claude\\\\plans\\\\ppt-180-brainstorming-playful-finch.md\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"plan\":\"# 医疗治疗仪行业·阿里国际站市场分析PPT\\n\\n## 背景\\n\\n- **客户行业**：医疗治疗仪（激光/红蓝光/经颅磁电治疗仪、检测仪、配件）\\n- **客户状态**：尚未入驻阿里国际站\\n- **使用场景**：客户经理上门面对面展示\\n- **PPT定位**：客观行业市场分析报告（不露转化意图）\\n- **语言**：中文\\n\\n## 数据来源\\n\\n| 数据类型 | 来源 | 谁做 |\\n|---------|------|:---:|\\n| 竞品店铺公开信息 | alibaba.com 搜索 | 我（WebSearch + WebFetch） |\\n| 竞品GMV数据 | 店铺名片悬停卡片 | 用户截图 |\\n| 行业趋势/地域分布 | 公开报告+海关数据 | 我 |\\n| 平台方向 | 阿里国际站官方 | 我 |\\n\\n## PPT结构（11页，中文）\\n\\n### Part 1：市场机会\\n1. 封面 — 医疗治疗仪行业·阿里国际站市场分析\\n2. 核心摘要 — 关键发现一页概览\\n3. 品类机会分析 — 子类目搜索热度/竞争度对比\\n4. 全球买家地域分析 — Top采购国分布\\n\\n### Part 2：竞争格局\\n5. 竞品总览 — 类目店铺概况\\n6-8. 对标店铺分析（3-4家各一页） — 截图占位+数据解读\\n9. 竞品汇总对比表\\n\\n### Part 3：行业前景\\n10. 国际站医疗行业趋势 — 平台方向/增长数据\\n11. 市场机遇总结\\n\\n## 执行流程\\n\\n### 阶段1：数据搜索（accio work 三路并行）\\n\\n```\\n                    ┌─ 路1：竞品店铺搜索 ──────→ 竞品数据\\n主任务 ── multi-* ──┼─ 路2：行业趋势+地域数据 ──→ 市场数据\\n                    └─ 路3：平台政策+医疗方向 ──→ 平台数据\\n```\\n\\n三路并行搜索，互不依赖，汇总后统一写PPT。\\n\\n### 阶段2：PPT生成\\n用 python-pptx 将汇总数据生成 11 页中文PPT，截图位置留占位框。\\n\\n### 阶段3：用户补充\\n用户打开PPT → 拖入竞品名片截图 → 完成。\\n\\n## 产出\\n\\n`D:\\\\ClaudeWorkspace\\\\05_PPT\\\\医疗治疗仪_国际站市场分析.pptx`\\n\\n## 验证\\n\\n- 11页完整 / 占位框标注清晰 / 中文表达 / 数据有出处\\n\",\"isAgent\":false,\"filePath\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\Administrator\\\\.claude\\\\plans\\\\ppt-180-brainstorming-playful-finch.md\",\"planWasEdited\":true}"</outcome>

  <parameters>"{\"description\":\"搜索竞品店铺数据\",\"prompt\":\"## 任务：搜索阿里国际站医疗治疗仪类目竞品店铺\\n\\n请在 alibaba.com 上搜索以下关键词，找到医疗治疗仪类目的竞品供应商店铺，提取公开可见的数据：\\n\\n### 搜索关键词\\n1. `laser therapy device`（激光治疗仪）\\n2. `red blue light therapy device`（红蓝光治疗仪）\\n3. `transcranial magnetic stimulation device`（经颅磁治疗仪）\\n4. `physical therapy equipment`（物理治疗设备）\\n\\n### 每条搜索结果需要提取的信息\\n对于每个找到的供应商店铺：\\n- 店铺名称\\n- 店铺链接\\n- 经营年限（如 \\\"10 YRS\\\"）\\n- 星等级（如有显示）\\n- 认证标识（如 Verified Supplier, Trade Assurance）\\n- 主营产品线\\n- 响应时间\\n- 店铺评分\\n- 任何可见的GMV或交易数据\\n\\n### 额外搜索\\n- 搜索 `medical therapy device manufacturer alibaba` 找更多竞品\\n- 尝试搜索阿里巴巴国际站医疗设备行业报告或公开数据\\n\\n请：\\n1. 先用 WebSearch 在 google 上搜索 \\\"alibaba medical therapy device supplier top sellers\\\"\\n2. 再用 WebFetch 直接抓取 alibaba.com 相关搜索结果页\\n3. 整理出至少 5-8 家有代表性的竞品店铺清单\\n\\n输出：竞品店铺清单表格，包含上述所有公开信息。\",\"subagent_type\":\"Explore\",\"run_in_background\":true}"</parameters>

Registration & Certification\\n- Sellers must hold **valid registration certificates** (e.g., China NMPA registration/certification) for all medical device products.\\n- For **cross-border sales**: Sellers must hold the relevant **destination-country-specific qualifications** (e.g., FDA Registration Number + FDA Product Code for the US; MDEL/MDL for Canada; CE marking under MDD/MDR for the EU).\\n- Certificates must be uploaded via: **MA Backend → Gold Supplier Center/Certification Center → Certificate Management → Certificate Upload**, selecting the \\\"Certification & Testing\\\" category.\\n\\n### B. Brand Authorization\\n- Sellers must have **legitimate brand authorization** from the brand owner to sell on the platform. Unauthorized sales are treated as infringement.\\n\\n### C. Product Listing Requirements\\n- **Product descriptions** must be detailed and accurate: covering functions, intended use, usage instructions, and precautions. Destination-Specific Qualification Systems\\n- **USA**: FDA Registration Number + FDA Product Code (each code requires separate platform submission).\\n- **Canada**: MDEL (Medical Device Establishment Licence) or MDL (Medical Device Licence); must include Company ID + \\\"HC\\\" + Licence Number.\\n- **EU**: CE certificate under MDD directive or MDR regulation + Declaration of Conformity (DOC). For Class I devices (non-sterile, non-reusable, non-measuring), manufacturer self-declaration may be accepted.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Prohibited & Restricted Actions\\n\\nAlibaba.com **prohibits**:\\n- Selling medical devices deemed high-risk by regulatory authorities\\n- Selling products subject to recalls or safety warnings\\n- Selling to a destination country without valid local qualifications\\n- Evading platform controls (hiding/masking product info, mis-categorizing products, etc.)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. Penalties for Non-Compliance\\n\\nViolations may result in:\\n- Product delisting / removal\\n- Product points deduction\\n- Store points deduction\\n- Restriction of product listing/selling functions\\n- Country-level selling bans\\n- **Account closure**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. Platform Monitoring\\n\\nAlibaba conducts **random inspections**, text/image recognition monitoring, and a \\\"mystery buyer\\\" quality testing program. Sellers are advised to regularly check their \\\"Violation Center\\\" for notices, as the platform may remove high-risk products **without prior announcement**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n> **Note:** Policies are updated regularly as regulations evolve. $726M (2024) |\\n| 5 | **France** | $667 million |\\n| 6 | **Canada** | $638 million |\\n| 7 | **United Kingdom** | $615 million |\\n| 8 | **South Korea** | $497 million |\\n| 9 | **Australia** | $525 million |\\n| 10 | **China** | $450 million |\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Key Regional Insights\\n\\n- **North America** is the largest regional market, dominated by the United States with over $6 billion in annual imports, followed by Canada and Mexico.\\n- **Europe** collectively represents a major import region, led by Germany, the Netherlands, France, the UK, Spain, Italy, and Belgium.\\n- **Asia-Pacific** is the fastest-growing region, with Japan, South Korea, China, Singapore, India, Vietnam, and Australia all ranking among significant importers.\\n- **Other notable importers** include Russia (~$250–338M), Switzerland (~$211M), Poland (~$183M), Brazil (~$149M), and Vietnam (~$122M).\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Key Drivers of Import Demand\\n\\n- **Aging populations** (particularly in Japan, Germany, and other developed nations)\\n- **Rising prevalence of musculoskeletal and neurological disorders**\\n- **Expanding healthcare infrastructure** (especially in Vietnam, India, and Southeast Asia)\\n- **Growing sports medicine and rehabilitation sectors**\\n- **Technological advancements** and demand for advanced electrotherapy, ultrasound, shockwave, and laser therapy devices\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Trade Flow Snapshot (UK Example)\\n\\nFor reference, the UK's top import sources for massage and physiotherapy devices (HS 901910) include:\\n- China: ~£210 million\\n- United States: ~£39 million\\n- Netherlands: ~£37 million\\n- Germany: ~£16 million\\n- Sweden: ~£15 million\\n\\nThese figures highlight China's dominance as a global supplier, while major developed economies remain the largest consumers of imported physical therapy equipment.\"],\"durationSeconds\":24.931352699999696}"</outcome>

Market Overview (Global Context)\\n\\n- The **global medical laser market** was valued at approximately **USD 6.5–6.9 billion in 2024–2025** and is projected to reach **USD 12–14.9 billion by 2033–2035**, with a CAGR of **5.8–8.1%**.\\n- The **diode/semiconductor laser segment** alone was valued at **USD 3.56 billion in 2024**, growing at **4.9% CAGR** toward USD 6 billion by 2035.\\n- Key demand drivers across all regions: rising preference for **minimally invasive procedures**, expansion of **aesthetic and dermatology treatments**, growing **ophthalmology needs**, and healthcare infrastructure modernization.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. Middle East & Africa (MEA)\\n\\n### Current Status & Growth Outlook\\n- MEA is described as a **moderate-growth region**—not as fast as Asia-Pacific but with steady, meaningful expansion.\\n- The market is primarily driven by **GCC countries** (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain), **Israel**, and **South Africa**.\\n- MEA faces some challenges around healthcare infrastructure and resource availability compared to developed markets, but **rising investments in medical technology** are fueling import demand.\\n\\n### Key Country-Level Insights\\n| Country | Key Drivers |\\n|---|---|\\n| **Saudi Arabia** | Vision 2030 healthcare transformation; large-scale hospital projects; growing medical tourism |\\n| **UAE** | Advanced medical tourism hub (Dubai/Abu Dhabi); high demand for aesthetic laser devices |\\n| **Israel** | Strong medical device innovation ecosystem; high adoption of advanced laser technologies |\\n| **South Africa** | Leading Sub-Saharan market; demand for surgical and ophthalmic lasers in public and private sectors |\\n| **Rest of MEA (Egypt, Nigeria, Iran)** | Emerging demand as healthcare access expands; price sensitivity is higher |\\n\\n### High-Demand Applications in MEA\\n- **Aesthetic/dermatology lasers** (hair removal, skin resurfacing, tattoo removal) — dominant in GCC\\n- **Ophthalmology lasers** (refractive surgery, cataract treatment)\\n- **Surgical lasers** (urology, gynecology, general surgery)\\n- **Dental lasers** — growing niche\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Southeast Asia\\n\\n### Current Status & Growth Outlook\\n- Southeast Asia is part of the **fastest-growing Asia-Pacific region**, projected to witness the **highest growth rate globally**.\\n- Drivers: **rising disposable incomes**, **expanding healthcare infrastructure**, **booming medical tourism** (Thailand, Malaysia, Singapore), and **large patient populations**.\\n- Markets tracked individually include: **Indonesia, Thailand, Philippines, Malaysia, Singapore, Vietnam**.\\n\\n### Key Country-Level Insights\\n| Country | Key Drivers |\\n|---|---|\\n| **Singapore** | Advanced healthcare hub; high-end surgical and ophthalmic laser demand |\\n| **Malaysia** | Growing medical tourism; expanding private hospital sector |\\n| **Thailand** | Medical tourism leader; strong demand for aesthetic & dermatology lasers |\\n| **Indonesia** | Largest population; rapidly expanding universal healthcare (BPJS); high volume potential |\\n| **Vietnam** | Fast-growing middle class; private clinic expansion; price-competitive segment |\\n| **Philippines** | Growing dermatology/aesthetics market; hospital modernization programs |\\n\\n### High-Demand Applications in Southeast Asia\\n- **Aesthetic lasers** — medical tourism-fueled demand in Thailand, Malaysia, Singapore\\n- **Ophthalmology lasers** — increasing cataract and refractive surgery volumes\\n- **Dental lasers** — rising private dental clinic adoption\\n- **Surgical lasers** — hospital modernization across Indonesia, Vietnam, Philippines\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. Comparative Regional Dynamics\\n\\n| Factor | Middle East (GCC) | Africa (Sub-Saharan) | Southeast Asia |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| **Growth Rate** | Moderate | Slow-to-Moderate | High (fastest globally) |\\n| **Price Sensitivity** | Low (premium devices) | High | Medium (varies by country) |\\n| **Key Segment** | Aesthetics & Ophthalmology | Surgical & Ophthalmic | Aesthetics & Dental |\\n| **Regulatory Hurdles** | Moderate (country-specific) | Moderate-High | Varies (harmonized in ASEAN) |\\n| **Medical Tourism Impact** | High (Dubai, Abu Dhabi) | Low | Very High (Thailand, Malaysia, Singapore) |\\n| **Primary Import Partners** | Europe, US, South Korea | China, Europe, India | China, Japan, South Korea, Europe, US |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. Strategic Takeaways for Market Entry\\n\\n1. **Southeast Asia offers the strongest growth trajectory** — prioritize Indonesia (volume) and Thailand/Malaysia (premium medical tourism segments).\\n2. **GCC countries represent the highest-value per-unit market** — Saudi Arabia and UAE are strong targets for premium diode and solid-state laser systems.\\n3. **South Africa is the gateway to Sub-Saharan Africa** — but expect longer sales cycles and higher price sensitivity.\\n4. **Aesthetic/dermatology lasers** are the most in-demand product category across all three regions.\\n5. **Diode lasers** dominate the global product mix due to versatility, compactness, and cost-efficiency — ideal for emerging market imports.\\n6. **Southeast Asia (ASEAN)** benefits from relatively harmonized medical device regulations, making it easier to register and distribute compared to the fragmented MEA regulatory landscape.\\n\\n---\\n\\n> **Note**: For granular data (specific import values by country, competitor market shares, pricing trends), specialized syndicated reports from sources like Fortune Business Insights, Polaris Market Research, Straits Research, or WiseGuy Reports would provide country-level revenue forecasts and import statistics. 4 Megatrends\",\"url\":\"https://commercetools.com/blog/what-is-next-in-healthcare-ecommerce?ref=blog.genalpha.com\"}]},\"# B2B Medical Device Ecommerce Trends 2025–2026\\n\\nHere's a comprehensive synthesis of the key trends shaping the B2B medical device ecommerce landscape, drawn from multiple market research reports and industry analyses.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. Massive Market Growth\\n\\n| Metric | Value |\\n|---|---|\\n| **Global healthcare e-commerce market (2025)** | ~$500–513 billion |\\n| **Projected by 2035** | $1.1–3.2 trillion |\\n| **CAGR** | ~17–18% |\\n| **Global e-commerce medical devices market (2025)** | $141.6 billion |\\n| **Projected by 2032** | $225.9 billion |\\n| **Medical device ecommerce CAGR** | ~7.0% |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. Key Strategic Trends\\n\\n### 🔄 B2B Is the Real Transformation Engine\\nWhile D2C gets the headlines, the **real structural change is in B2B**. Hospitals, labs, clinics, and provider networks now demand the same transparency, predictability, and self-service capabilities expected in other B2B industries — but with healthcare-specific constraints:\\n- Negotiated/account-based pricing\\n- Regulated products requiring credentialing\\n- Entitlement-driven catalogs\\n- Recurring but rarely identical orders\\n- Long-term service relationships tied to each transaction\\n\\n### 🏗️ Commerce Shifts from \\\"Channel\\\" to \\\"Infrastructure\\\"\\nLeading organizations are treating commerce not as a front-end website refresh but as **core infrastructure** touching pricing engines, ERP, inventory, compliance, service workflows, and analytics. The key question: *not \\\"can we sell online?\\\"* but *\\\"how do orders flow cleanly from click to fulfillment?\\\"*\\n\\n### 🤖 AI Is Reshaping Operations\\nAI has moved from experimental to **core operational infrastructure** across B2B medical device ecommerce:\\n- AI-enabled diagnostics and IoT-connected monitoring devices create new service layers\\n- Intelligent search, personalized recommendations, and automated customer service\\n- Predictive maintenance on medical devices\\n- Automated regulatory compliance checks\\n\\n### 🌐 Digital-First Procurement Goes Mainstream\\nDigital procurement models are replacing legacy channel structures. Self-service portals, marketplace integration, and real-time inventory visibility are becoming baseline expectations — not differentiators. \\\"Digital-first\\\" is no longer optional even in regulated B2B environments.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Regulatory & Compliance Pressures\\n\\n- **Compliance is now the operating floor**, not a ceiling. Regulators (FDA, NMPA, EU MDR) are intensifying scrutiny of online distribution of medical devices.\\n- New rules around **software updates, cybersecurity, and post-market surveillance** apply directly to ecommerce channels.\\n- Platforms must embed **automated credential/gating verification, quality traceability (GSP/GMP), and real-time compliance monitoring** into their core architecture.\\n- Penalties are escalating — e.g., China's updated regulations allow fines up to **30x the product value** for quality/safety violations.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. Supply Chain & Tariff Pressures\\n\\n- **US tariff policy shifts through 2025** have materially impacted landed costs for devices relying on globalized manufacturing.\\n- Responses include: **nearshoring, supplier diversification, shifting inventory closer to demand centers**, and renegotiating commercial terms with ecommerce partners.\\n- Channel managers are adjusting SKU strategies across D2C and hospital procurement portals to reflect differential duties.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. B2B-Specific Platform Innovations\\n\\n- **Platforms like eZRx+ (Zuellig Pharma)** are launching purpose-built B2B healthcare purchasing platforms for pharmacies and clinics — offering ordering, payments, and returns management in one digital experience.\\n- **Salesforce B2B Commerce and similar tools** gain traction because they model healthcare complexity (contract pricing, entitlement catalogs, role-based access, CRM integration) rather than abstract it away.\\n- Case study: **Ovation Medical** rebuilt on B2B Commerce and reduced operational risk, improved data quality, and scaled without adding headcount.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. Consumer-Grade Expectations Invade B2B\\n\\nA critical trend: **B2B buyers bring consumer expectations with them** — clarity, speed, real-time feedback, intuitive UX. Usability is no longer a \\\"nice to have\\\" in B2B medical device commerce. Procurement managers and clinicians alike expect the same ease of use they get from Amazon or Alibaba.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 7. Regional Dynamics\\n\\n| Region | Key Dynamic |\\n|---|---|\\n| **Americas** | Mature digital infrastructure; strong D2C appetite; scalable fulfillment partnerships and integrated reimbursement pathways |\\n| **Europe, Middle East & Africa** | Mosaic of regulatory regimes; requires localized compliance, multilingual support, adaptable distribution |\\n| **Asia-Pacific** | Fastest digital adoption; expansive consumer platforms; China's medical device market exceeds ¥1.3 trillion with online channels driving core growth (O2O instant retail up 120% YoY) |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Bottom Line\\n\\nThe B2B medical device ecommerce space in 2025–2026 is defined by **structural transformation, not incremental improvement**. The winners will be organizations that treat commerce as core infrastructure, embed compliance into digital operations, leverage AI operationally, and meet B2B buyers' rising expectations for consumer-grade digital experiences — all while navigating tariff pressures and fragmented global regulations.\"],\"durationSeconds\":40.70190630000038}"</outcome>

2033\",\"url\":\"https://www.giiresearch.com/report/grvi1842015-transcranial-magnetic-stimulation-system-market.html\"}]},\"# 经颅磁刺激（TMS）设备市场增长 —— 搜索结果摘要\\n\\n## 市场规模概览\\n\\n不同研究机构对TMS市场的估值略有差异，但整体均显示强劲增长态势：\\n\\n| 数据来源 | 基准年规模 | 预测年规模 | CAGR |\\n|---------|-----------|-----------|------|\\n| **TBRC** | 2025年 $13.9亿 | 2030年 $22.1亿 | **9.8%** |\\n| **OG Analysis** | 2025年 $17亿 | 2034年 $36亿 | **8.6%** |\\n| **Expert Market Research** | 2024年 $13亿 | 2034年 $32.5亿 | **9.6%** |\\n| **Grand View Research** | 2024年 $2.57亿(系统) | 2033年 $5.94亿 | **9.89%** |\\n| **QYResearch** | 2025年 ¥14.29亿 | 2032年 ¥39.84亿 | **16.0%** |\\n| **Verified Market Research** | 2024年 $11.3亿 | 2032年 $21.6亿 | **8.45%** |\\n\\n> 注：规模差异主要源于统计口径不同（仅设备 vs 设备+系统+服务）。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 核心增长驱动因素\\n\\n1. **精神/神经系统疾病患病率上升**：全球约2.8亿人患抑郁症（WHO），约5000万人患癫痫，焦虑症、强迫症（OCD）、帕金森病、阿尔茨海默病等发病率持续增加。\\n\\n2. **FDA/CE监管审批持续扩展**：\\n   - 2024年3月：NeuroStar获FDA批准用于15-21岁青少年MDD辅助治疗\\n   - 2024年8月：Neurocare iTBS方案获FDA批准\\n   - 2024年11月：Magstim Horizon Inspire系统获FDA批准\\n   - 2025年7月：升级版Magstim Rapid研究系统获FDA批准\\n\\n3. **非药物疗法认知提升**：患者和医生对非侵入性治疗的接受度不断提高，TMS作为药物耐受性差患者的替代方案需求强劲。\\n\\n4. **保险覆盖扩展**：欧美地区TMS报销政策逐步完善，降低了患者经济负担。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 主要市场趋势\\n\\n- **AI个性化治疗**：AI驱动的TMS协议可根据患者脑电波数据实时调整刺激参数，治疗效果提升约40%\\n- **便携/家用TMS设备**：小型化设备配合远程监测，突破临床场景限制\\n- **深度TMS (dTMS)**：Brainsway的H线圈等技术可刺激更深脑区，拓展OCD、成瘾等适应症\\n- **神经导航引导**：结合fMRI/EEG提高精确定位精度（如MagVenture Atlas系统）\\n- **新适应症拓展**：阿尔茨海默病（3项III期试验进行中）、中风康复、偏头痛、自闭症等领域探索\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 区域分布\\n\\n| 区域 | 市场份额及特点 |\\n|------|--------------|\\n| **中国** | 全球最大市场（~40%份额），2018-2024年CAGR高达33%，2024年市场规模¥8.63亿 |\\n| **北美** | ~25%份额，监管环境成熟、保险覆盖好，主导高端市场 |\\n| **欧洲** | ~20%份额，技术应用成熟 |\\n| **亚太(除中国)** | 最具增长潜力，医疗投资加大，日本、韩国关注认知增强与老年心理健康 |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 主要竞争格局\\n\\n全球前三强占超40%份额：**依瑞德**(中国)、**Magstim**(英国)、**Neuronetics**(美国)\\n\\n其他重要企业：MagVenture(丹麦)、Brainsway(以色列)、Nexstim(芬兰)、Neurosoft(俄罗斯)、伟思医疗(中国)、英智科技(中国)、eNeura等。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 主要挑战\\n\\n- ⚠️ 设备及疗程费用高昂（单台设备价格较高，需多次治疗）\\n- ⚠️ 部分地区保险覆盖不足\\n- ⚠️ 专业操作人员培训不足\\n- ⚠️ 新兴市场认知度和可及性有限\\n- ⚠️ 关税影响进口磁线圈和电子元件成本\\n\\n---\\n\\n**总结**：全球TMS设备市场正处于稳健增长期，预计到2030-2034年市场规模将达20-36亿美元，整体CAGR在8-10%左右（中国市场增速更快，超过16%）。增长动力主要来自精神疾病负担加重、技术迭代创新、监管审批扩展以及非侵入性治疗理念的普及。\"],\"durationSeconds\":41.39226580000017}"</outcome>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Competitor intelligence gathered: key Alibaba medical laser/TMS/physical therapy suppliers identified</title>
  <subtitle>Web searches surfaced 10+ competitor stores with ratings, years, revenue data, and product pricing across three device categories</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Sunnyou Medical (三友医疗): 17 years on Alibaba, 4.7/5.0 rating, 95.7% satisfaction, SGS certified, top seller rank #4 in traction/correction category</fact>
    <fact>Laser therapy devices on Alibaba range from $50-400 (handheld) to $600-1,500+ (professional clinical), with Wuhan, Hubei as primary manufacturing cluster</fact>
    <fact>TMS device market: Shenzhen is dominant hub (70%+ of global TMS suppliers), Sunnyou/Sunnymed SY-S852 at ¥1,410-7,244, Kai Cheng at ~$9,100 with DDP shipping</fact>
    <fact>Physical therapy top suppliers: Shenzhen Konmed (5.0 rating, 100% delivery, 12yr), Shenzhen Roundwhale (5.0, 42% reorder rate - highest), Guangzhou Genwave ($560K+ revenue, ≤1hr response)</fact>
    <fact>Rhein Laser Technologies: 4.6 rating, $1.4M+ annual online revenue, 20% reorder rate</fact>
    <fact>Laser therapy device market: $2.37B (2024), $2.49B (2025), 5.2% CAGR to $3.74B by 2033; broader medical laser systems: $5.35B (2024) at 11.5% CAGR</fact>
    <fact>Physical therapy equipment top importing countries: US ($5.7-6.5B), Germany ($1.08B), Japan ($870M), Netherlands ($1.1B), France ($667M)</fact>
    <fact>China medical device exports Jan-Aug 2025: +6.84% YoY, trade surplus +103.58% to ¥842B RMB, BRI countries +13.35% growth</fact>
    <fact>Global medical device market 2024: $617.6B, 6.37% YoY growth; China exports $35.34B Jan-Sep 2024 (+4.4%)</fact>
    <fact>Alibaba medical device seller requirements: NMPA registration, FDA/CE per destination country, quality inspection reports, brand authorization, strict compliance with country-specific qualification systems</fact>
    <fact>Alibaba key growth categories for 2025: dental equipment (largest), physical therapy/rehab, ultrasound/optical, surgical instruments, smart diagnostics, elderly rehab, home monitoring</fact>
    <fact>China medical equipment exports to 190+ countries, covering 9,000+ medical institutions, 9.4% 5-year CAGR</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>
    Phase 1 data collection is yielding substantial structured intelligence across all three parallel search paths. Competitor store data reveals clear market leaders: Sunnyou Medical (17yr veteran, 95.7% satisfaction) dominates laser therapy, while Shenzhen-based suppliers (Konmed, Roundwhale, XFT) lead physical therapy with perfect 5.0 ratings. The TMS segment is highly concentrated in Shenzhen. Market sizing data shows the medical therapy device market at $2.4-5.8B depending on segment definition, with 5-12% CAGR. Import data confirms US as dominant buyer ($5.7-6.5B), while China's export growth is accelerating (+7.05% in 2024, +6.84% in 2025 partial). Alibaba platform data reveals strict regulatory requirements for medical device sellers (NMPA + destination-country certifications like FDA/CE) and identifies dental equipment and physical therapy as fastest-growing categories.
  </narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>
</observation>

Key Strategic Trends\\n\\n### 🔄 B2B Is the Real Transformation Engine\\nWhile D2C gets the headlines, the **real structural change is in B2B**. Self-service portals, marketplace integration, and real-time inventory visibility are becoming baseline expectations — not differentiators. \\\"Digital-first\\\" is no longer optional even in regulated B2B environments.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Regulatory & Compliance Pressures\\n\\n- **Compliance is now the operating floor**, not a ceiling. Regulators (FDA, NMPA, EU MDR) are intensifying scrutiny of online distribution of medical devices.\\n- New rules around **software updates, cybersecurity, and post-market surveillance** apply directly to ecommerce channels.\\n- Platforms must embed **automated credential/gating verification, quality traceability (GSP/GMP), and real-time compliance monitoring** into their core architecture.\\n- Penalties are escalating — e.g., China's updated regulations allow fines up to **30x the product value** for quality/safety violations.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. Supply Chain & Tariff Pressures\\n\\n- **US tariff policy shifts through 2025** have materially impacted landed costs for devices relying on globalized manufacturing.\\n- Responses include: **nearshoring, supplier diversification, shifting inventory closer to demand centers**, and renegotiating commercial terms with ecommerce partners.\\n- Channel managers are adjusting SKU strategies across D2C and hospital procurement portals to reflect differential duties.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. B2B-Specific Platform Innovations\\n\\n- **Platforms like eZRx+ (Zuellig Pharma)** are launching purpose-built B2B healthcare purchasing platforms for pharmacies and clinics — offering ordering, payments, and returns management in one digital experience.\\n- **Salesforce B2B Commerce and similar tools** gain traction because they model healthcare complexity (contract pricing, entitlement catalogs, role-based access, CRM integration) rather than abstract it away.\\n- Case study: **Ovation Medical** rebuilt on B2B Commerce and reduced operational risk, improved data quality, and scaled without adding headcount.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. Usability is no longer a \\\"nice to have\\\" in B2B medical device commerce. 2033\",\"url\":\"https://www.giiresearch.com/report/grvi1842015-transcranial-magnetic-stimulation-system-market.html\"}]},\"# 经颅磁刺激（TMS）设备市场增长 —— 搜索结果摘要\\n\\n## 市场规模概览\\n\\n不同研究机构对TMS市场的估值略有差异，但整体均显示强劲增长态势：\\n\\n| 数据来源 | 基准年规模 | 预测年规模 | CAGR |\\n|---------|-----------|-----------|------|\\n| **TBRC** | 2025年 $13.9亿 | 2030年 $22.1亿 | **9.8%** |\\n| **OG Analysis** | 2025年 $17亿 | 2034年 $36亿 | **8.6%** |\\n| **Expert Market Research** | 2024年 $13亿 | 2034年 $32.5亿 | **9.6%** |\\n| **Grand View Research** | 2024年 $2.57亿(系统) | 2033年 $5.94亿 | **9.89%** |\\n| **QYResearch** | 2025年 ¥14.29亿 | 2032年 ¥39.84亿 | **16.0%** |\\n| **Verified Market Research** | 2024年 $11.3亿 | 2032年 $21.6亿 | **8.45%** |\\n\\n> 注：规模差异主要源于统计口径不同（仅设备 vs 设备+系统+服务）。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 核心增长驱动因素\\n\\n1. **精神/神经系统疾病患病率上升**：全球约2.8亿人患抑郁症（WHO），约5000万人患癫痫，焦虑症、强迫症（OCD）、帕金森病、阿尔茨海默病等发病率持续增加。\\n\\n2. **FDA/CE监管审批持续扩展**：\\n   - 2024年3月：NeuroStar获FDA批准用于15-21岁青少年MDD辅助治疗\\n   - 2024年8月：Neurocare iTBS方案获FDA批准\\n   - 2024年11月：Magstim Horizon Inspire系统获FDA批准\\n   - 2025年7月：升级版Magstim Rapid研究系统获FDA批准\\n\\n3. **非药物疗法认知提升**：患者和医生对非侵入性治疗的接受度不断提高，TMS作为药物耐受性差患者的替代方案需求强劲。\\n\\n4. **保险覆盖扩展**：欧美地区TMS报销政策逐步完善，降低了患者经济负担。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 主要市场趋势\\n\\n- **AI个性化治疗**：AI驱动的TMS协议可根据患者脑电波数据实时调整刺激参数，治疗效果提升约40%\\n- **便携/家用TMS设备**：小型化设备配合远程监测，突破临床场景限制\\n- **深度TMS (dTMS)**：Brainsway的H线圈等技术可刺激更深脑区，拓展OCD、成瘾等适应症\\n- **神经导航引导**：结合fMRI/EEG提高精确定位精度（如MagVenture Atlas系统）\\n- **新适应症拓展**：阿尔茨海默病（3项III期试验进行中）、中风康复、偏头痛、自闭症等领域探索\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 区域分布\\n\\n| 区域 | 市场份额及特点 |\\n|------|--------------|\\n| **中国** | 全球最大市场（~40%份额），2018-2024年CAGR高达33%，2024年市场规模¥8.63亿 |\\n| **北美** | ~25%份额，监管环境成熟、保险覆盖好，主导高端市场 |\\n| **欧洲** | ~20%份额，技术应用成熟 |\\n| **亚太(除中国)** | 最具增长潜力，医疗投资加大，日本、韩国关注认知增强与老年心理健康 |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 主要竞争格局\\n\\n全球前三强占超40%份额：**依瑞德**(中国)、**Magstim**(英国)、**Neuronetics**(美国)\\n\\n其他重要企业：MagVenture(丹麦)、Brainsway(以色列)、Nexstim(芬兰)、Neurosoft(俄罗斯)、伟思医疗(中国)、英智科技(中国)、eNeura等。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 主要挑战\\n\\n- ⚠️ 设备及疗程费用高昂（单台设备价格较高，需多次治疗）\\n- ⚠️ 部分地区保险覆盖不足\\n- ⚠️ 专业操作人员培训不足\\n- ⚠️ 新兴市场认知度和可及性有限\\n- ⚠️ 关税影响进口磁线圈和电子元件成本\\n\\n---\\n\\n**总结**：全球TMS设备市场正处于稳健增长期，预计到2030-2034年市场规模将达20-36亿美元，整体CAGR在8-10%左右（中国市场增速更快，超过16%）。增长动力主要来自精神疾病负担加重、技术迭代创新、监管审批扩展以及非侵入性治疗理念的普及。\"],\"durationSeconds\":41.39226580000017}"</outcome>

App Store\",\"url\":\"https://apps.apple.com/cv/app/qunamai/id6748783795\"}]},\"## 医疗器械跨境电商（中国出口 & 阿里巴巴B2B）关键信息汇总\\n\\n以下是基于搜索结果的综合梳理：\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 一、中国医疗器械出口概况\\n\\n| 指标 | 数据 |\\n|------|------|\\n| 2024年全球医疗器械市场规模 | **6,230亿美元** |\\n| 2024年中国医疗器械出口额 | **428亿美元**（占全球7.8%） |\\n| 对东盟出口额 | **243.6亿元**，同比增长26.2% |\\n| \\\"一带一路\\\"沿线出口 | **659亿元**（2024年），年复合增长18.4% |\\n\\n中国医疗器械正从\\\"跟跑者\\\"向\\\"并行者\\\"\\\"领跑者\\\"转变，高端设备渗透率从2020年的12%提升至2024年的23%。\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 二、主要B2B平台\\n\\n1. **阿里巴巴国际站（Alibaba.com）** — 最主流平台，已有大量成功案例（如安庆市医用耗材通过阿里国际站以\\\"9710\\\"模式出口韩国）。\\n2. **Global Sources（环球资源）**\\n3. **Made-in-China（中国制造网）**\\n4. **Med-shopping.com** — 垂直医疗跨境B2B平台\\n5. **数商云** — 提供\\\"智能采购+合规管理+物流协同\\\"一体化方案\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 三、阿里巴巴国际站销售医疗器械的核心合规要求\\n\\n#### 欧盟市场（CE认证）：\\n- 符合 **MDD指令**（医疗器械指令）或 **MDR法规**（医疗器械法规）的CE证书\\n- 需提交 **符合性声明（DOC）**\\n- 普通I类产品可自我声明，无需公告机构介入\\n- 涉及品类：医用口罩、防护服、血氧仪、红外测温仪、呼吸机、病员监护仪等\\n\\n#### 美国市场：\\n- 需 **FDA 510(k)认证**（平均耗时10-12个月）\\n- 高风险器械需提交临床数据\\n\\n#### 其他认证要求：\\n- ISO 13485 质量管理体系\\n- 目标国注册证书/许可证\\n- EPR（生产者延伸责任）合规（法国等市场）\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 四、跨境电商B2B通关模式\\n\\n| 模式 | 代码 | 说明 |\\n|------|------|------|\\n| 直接出口 | **9710** | 企业通过跨境物流直接向境外企业出口 |\\n| 出口海外仓 | **9810** | 先出口至海外仓，再通过平台完成交易 |\\n\\n优势：相比一般贸易，B2B出口申报手续更简化，物流查验更便捷，通关效率提高，出口成本降低。\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 五、成功案例\\n\\n- **广州创业团队**：专注医疗器械出海S2B，仅用两年成为阿里巴巴\\\"寻梦基地\\\"\\n- **迈瑞医疗**：通过海外仓布局和并购，2024年海外收入占比达48%\\n- **联影医疗**：影像设备已覆盖美国50%以上州级行政区\\n- **鱼跃医疗**：与亚马逊合作布局家用器械，跨境电商渠道收入增长60%\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 六、关键挑战与建议\\n\\n1. **合规壁垒**：CE-MDR认证周期10-18个月，FDA 510(k) 10-12个月，需提前规划\\n2. **供应链韧性**：冷链物流、海外仓布局至关重要\\n3. **本土化运营**：支付习惯（东南亚货到付款占比60%+）、文化适配\\n4. **平台规则**：不合规销售面临商品下架、国别屏蔽、关店等处罚\\n\\n如果您需要更深入了解某个方面（如特定品类资质要求、具体平台入驻流程、海关数据开发客户等），欢迎进一步提问！\"],\"durationSeconds\":29.154402199999893}"</outcome>

- Red/blue light therapy suppliers: AZURE, IDEARED, IDEATHERAPY, LEDEAST (12yr), Magique Power
- Rhine Laser Technologies: $1.4M+ revenue

2. Industry trends & market data (Agent 2):
- Laser therapy market: $2.37B (2024), 5.2% CAGR
- Medical laser systems: $5.35B (2024), 11.5% CAGR
- Red light therapy panels: $0.96B (2025), 15.2% CAGR → fastest growing
- TMS market: $13-17B (2024-2025), 8-10% CAGR, China 40% market share
- Physical therapy top importers: US $5.7-6.5B, Germany $1.08B, Japan $870M
- Regional analysis for Africa, Middle East, Southeast Asia
- B2B medical device ecommerce trends: healthcare e-commerce ~$500-513B (2025), 17-18% CAGR
- China medical device exports: $35.34B (Jan-Sep 2024, +4.4%), 190+ countries

3. Platform policy & Alibaba data (Agent 3):
- Strict medical device seller requirements (NMPA, FDA, CE per destination)
- Alibaba Health revenue: RMB 30.60B (FY2025, +13.2%), accelerating to +17% in H1 FY2026
- Alibaba medical categories: dental equipment largest, physical therapy/rehab growing
- Alibaba Health trending: CGM, home medical devices, TCM wellness export boom
- Success stories: SNJ ($5M overseas via Alibaba), Medtecs (Taiwan first 5-star), Guangzhou Yilu Jiuge (178 countries)
- 2025 key trends: smart diagnostics, elderly rehab, home monitoring, professional one-stop procurement

This is a LOT of data. Let me create several observations capturing the key findings.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Red/blue light therapy supplier landscape mapped: 5 key Alibaba manufacturers identified</title>
  <subtitle>AZURE (ISO13485), IDEARED (MDSAP), IDEATHERAPY, LEDEAST (12yr veteran), and Magique Power dominate Alibaba red light therapy</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>AZURE (Guangdong): ISO13485/CE/FCC/RoHS/ETL/UKCA certified, 2-3yr warranty, RHPro series 300W-3600W panels with optional 480nm blue light</fact>
    <fact>IDEARED (Guangdong): MDSAP/ISO13485/UL/ETL/SAA/UKCA/CE/RoHS, 3yr warranty, full-body LED beauty devices</fact>
    <fact>IDEATHERAPY: 9Wave RLPRO100 desktop panel with 9 wavelengths including 480nm blue, App smart control</fact>
    <fact>LEDEAST (Shenzhen): 12 years on Alibaba, LLD-002 model with 60×5W dual-chip LEDs at 660/850nm</fact>
    <fact>Magique Power (Zhengzhou): ISO13485/RoHS/CE/EMC/UL since 2011, 2yr warranty</fact>
    <fact>Pure red+blue combo devices are rare on Alibaba - most products are red+NIR only, with blue light as optional add-on</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>
    The red/blue light therapy device segment on Alibaba is dominated by Guangdong and Shenzhen manufacturers. AZURE and IDEARED stand out for their ISO13485 medical device certifications. A key insight for the PPT: true "red+blue light" combination devices are limited — most suppliers focus on red+NIR wavelengths, suggesting this specific niche could be an opportunity for the client. LEDEAST's 12-year tenure demonstrates market maturity in this category.
  </narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>discovery</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
  </concepts>
</observation>

    <fact>XFT online revenue: only $44K-54K+ (low), suggesting most sales through offline channels; products: AED trainers, FES foot drop systems, sEMG biofeedback</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>
    The final competitor verification round surfaced critical intelligence for the PPT. Azure Technology emerged as the most comprehensively certified red/blue light competitor (ISO13485 + FDA 510k + 8 certifications total). Most importantly, Guangyang Zhongkang was discovered to be using deceptive TMS/RTMS keywords — their "TMS devices" are actually 810nm LED photobiomodulation helmets, not genuine electromagnetic coil-based transcranial magnetic stimulators. This is significant competitive intelligence that could inform how the client should position authentic medical devices on Alibaba. XFT Medical shows an interesting disconnect: 21 years in business with perfect delivery but minimal Alibaba online revenue ($44-54K), suggesting they primarily use offline channels despite having an Alibaba presence.
  </narrative>

  <request>医疗治疗仪行业阿里国际站市场分析PPT — Phase 1数据采集阶段：三路并行搜索竞品店铺、行业市场数据、平台政策，所有数据路径均已返回</request>
  <investigated>
通过约40次WebSearch，三个并行Explore代理完成了全面的数据采集：(1)竞品店铺：alibaba.com上激光治疗仪、TMS、红蓝光、物理治疗四大品类的供应商店铺指标（年限、评分、交付率、复购率、年营收、出口市场）；(2)行业市场数据：全球医疗治疗仪市场规模（激光$2.37B/6.37B、红光$0.96B/15.2%CAGR、TMS $13-17B/8.6-9.8%CAGR、物理治疗$19.46B/6.1%CAGR）、中国出口数据（2024 ¥3612亿/+7%）、Top20进口国排名（HS 9018，美国$41.47B居首）；(3)阿里平台政策：医疗器械卖家入驻资质要求（NMPA+FDA/CE-MDR认证10-18月）、平台增长数据（FY2025收入¥306亿/+13.2%）、成功案例（SNJ $500万、Medtecs首家五星医械卖家等）
  </investigated>
  <learned>
关键发现：(1)红光治疗面板CAGR 15.2%是增速最快的细分品类；(2)TMS市场中国占全球40%份额，但需警惕Guangyang Zhongkang等供应商用"TMS"关键词实际销售LED光生物调节头盔（非真正经颅磁刺激设备）；(3)竞争对手呈现两种出口市场策略：Sunnyou Medical主攻新兴市场（哈萨克斯坦26%、菲律宾22%），Rhein Laser主攻美国（62%），说明不同市场策略均能在阿里国际站存活；(4)XFT Medical成立21年但在阿里仅3年且线上营收仅$44-54K，表明很多老牌医械企业仍以线下渠道为主，线上竞争尚未充分饱和；(5)阿里国际站医疗器械入驻壁垒高（认证周期10-18月），对新入卖家反而是护城河
  </investigated>
  <completed>
- 11页PPT整体方案已完成并获用户审批（plan文件：ppt-180-brainstorming-playful-finch.md）
- 三路并行数据搜索全部返回，覆盖竞品店铺（8家已验证详细档案）、市场数据（4个品类市场规模+CAGR+买家地域）、平台政策（入驻要求+合规+趋势）
- 输出路径确定：D:\ClaudeWorkspace\05_PPT\医疗治疗仪_国际站市场分析.pptx
- 用户已被告知可在PPT生成后拖入竞品名片截图
  </completed>
  <next_steps>
Phase 2即将启动：用python-pptx将汇总数据生成11页中文PPT。需要先安装python-pptx库，然后编写生成脚本。PPT将包含：Part 1市场机会（封面、核心摘要、品类机会、买家地域）、Part 2竞争格局（竞品总览、3-4家对标店铺单页分析、汇总对比表）、Part 3行业前景（平台趋势、市场机遇总结）。截图位置留占位框供用户后续拖入。
  </next_steps>
  <notes>
项目背景特殊：用户（母婴纺织品工厂运营）受国际站客户经理委托，为第三方医疗治疗仪客户制作转化用市场分析PPT。PPT定位为客观行业报告（不露转化意图），面向医疗专业人士的面对面展示场景。数据收集非常充分，远超PPT实际所需（可能只需其中30-40%的数据即可填满11页），需选最有冲击力的数据放入PPT。Guangyang Zhongkang的虚假TMS标签是意外发现，可作为PPT中"市场机会差异化"的论据。
  </notes>
</summary>

content.thinking: The competitor store search agent (af7fdaaaa8f5cd604) has completed and returned a comprehensive competitor analysis report. This is a significant milestone - all three paths of parallel search are now complete. Let me record the key structured findings from this final report.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Competitor store search agent completed: 24-store comprehensive analysis with four-tier competitive landscape</title>
  <subtitle>Explore agent returned structured competitor data across laser, red/blue light, TMS, and physical therapy categories</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Agent af7fdaaaa8f5cd604 completed: 24 competitor stores profiled across 4 product category tables (A-D)</fact>
    <fact>Four-tier competitive landscape identified: Tier 1 (Konmed 5.0/12yr, Roundwhale 5.0/42% reorder, Rhein Laser $1.1M), Tier 2 (Azure ISO13485+FDA, XFT 21yr, Sunnyou 17yr), Tier 3 (Genwave 4.9/≤1hr, Huangkai 4.8/14yr, HS Medical 4.9/100% delivery), Tier 4 (AmywellMED $428 TMS, Deen $440, COZING $59)</fact>
    <fact>Geographic clustering confirmed: Shenzhen (70%+ of high-end medical electronics), Wuhan (laser cluster), Henan (cost-advantage), Ningbo (low-cost portable)</fact>
    <fact>Price range analysis per category: laser $50-$19,600, red light $59-$3,600, TMS $428-$9,100, physical therapy $24.50-$1,000+</fact>
    <fact>Guangyang Zhongkang flagged as deceptive: markets PBM LED helmets as "TMS" — not genuine transcranial magnetic stimulation</fact>
    <fact>Shenzhen Osto Medical: 18yr, 11,000m² factory (largest), 150+ staff, 96.4% delivery</fact>
    <fact>Shenzhen Fitconn: 12yr, 27% reorder rate, 6,900m² factory, 97.6% delivery</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>
    The first Explore agent completed, delivering a comprehensive 24-store competitive landscape report organized into four product category tables. The report includes a four-tier competitive framework, geographic manufacturing cluster analysis, per-category price range comparison, and identification of deceptive marketing practices (Guangyang Zhongkang's fake TMS labeling). This report combined with the market data (Path 2) and platform policy data (Path 3) gives complete coverage for all 11 PPT slides. Phase 2 PPT generation can now proceed with all data in hand.
  </narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
</observation>
